column1: SOA-C02,"Domain 5: Networking & Content Delivery","Networking & Content Delivery","## SOA-C02 Domain 5: Networking & Content Delivery
This domain covers VPC configuration, connectivity options, DNS, content delivery, and network troubleshooting.

column3: Deny all in, allow all out

column1: - No transitive routing (A-B, B-C does NOT mean A-C)
- Must update route tables in both VPCs
- Supports: VPC, VPN, Direct Connect, peering

column3: Access multiple VPCs via Transit Gateway
Not encrypted by default (add VPN over DX)

column1: - Private DNS resolves service to private IP
- Threshold: Number of consecutive checks to change status
- String matching: Check response body for specific text
- CloudWatch Alarm: Health check based on CloudWatch metric

column3: Source of content (S3, ALB, EC2, custom)
Larger cache between edge and origin
Restrict S3 access to CloudFront only

column1: - **Cache Key:** What makes requests unique (headers, query strings, cookies)
- **Origin Request Policy:** What to forward to origin

column3: No public IP or IGW route
column4: Assign public IP + route to IGW
Add VPC Endpoint or NAT Gateway

column1: version account-id interface-id srcaddr dstaddr srcport dstport protocol packets bytes start end action log-status
- Does NOT capture: DNS, DHCP, metadata, license activation, mirror traffic
- **""Private subnet needs internet""** -> NAT Gateway in public subnet + route
- **""Block specific IP""** -> NACL deny rule (SGs cannot deny)
- **""Private access to S3""** -> VPC Gateway Endpoint (free)
- **""Private access to other services""** -> Interface Endpoint (PrivateLink)
- **""Encrypt Direct Connect""** -> VPN over Direct Connect
- **""Multiple on-prem sites""** -> CloudHub or Transit Gateway + VPN
- **""Access VPC from on-prem via endpoint""** -> Interface Endpoint (not Gateway)
- **""Route to lowest latency""** -> Latency routing policy
- **""A/B testing with traffic split""** -> Weighted routing
- **""Alias vs CNAME""** -> Alias for zone apex, free, AWS resources only
- **""Restrict S3 to CloudFront""** -> OAC (Origin Access Control)
- **""HTTPS for custom domain""** -> ACM certificate (us-east-1 for CloudFront)
- **""See why traffic is blocked""** -> VPC Flow Logs (REJECT entries)
- **""Test path without sending traffic""** -> Reachability Analyzer
- **NAT Gateway** is per-AZ. For HA, deploy one in each AZ with separate routes.
- **VPC Peering** is NOT transitive. A-B and B-C does not connect A-C.
- **Gateway Endpoints** (S3, DynamoDB) are free. Interface Endpoints cost money.
- **Gateway Endpoints** cannot be accessed from on-premises. Interface Endpoints can.
- **Direct Connect** is NOT encrypted by default. Layer a VPN on top for encryption.
- **Route 53 Alias** records are free, work at zone apex, and point to AWS resources.
- **CNAME** cannot be used at zone apex (example.com). Use Alias instead.
- **Failover routing** requires a health check on the primary record.
- **OAC** (Origin Access Control) replaces OAI for restricting S3 access to CloudFront.
- **VPC Flow Logs** show ACCEPT/REJECT but NOT packet content.
- **Reachability Analyzer** tests connectivity paths without sending actual traffic.
- **5 IPs reserved per subnet** (.0, .1, .2, .3, .255). A /24 has 251 usable IPs.
- **Security Groups are stateful** (return traffic auto-allowed). NACLs are stateless."
